Merrill Gardens at Rockridge

Part of Merrill Gardens · Oakland, Alameda County, California

  • Independent Living
  • Assisted Living
  • Memory Care

Contact & Location

Address
5238 Coronado Ave, Oakland, CA 94618 · Map
Phone
(510) 338-4543
Website
merrillgardens.com

About Merrill Gardens at Rockridge

Newer senior living community in Oakland's Rockridge neighborhood offering independent living, assisted living, and memory care, with architecture designed to match the classic character of the area. Located close to the dining and retail of College Avenue and BART. Reviewers highlight the bright, modern building, rooftop garden spaces, and active social calendar.

Care offered here

  • Independent Living — what independent living includes and who it fits
  • Assisted Living — what assisted living includes and who it fits
  • Memory Care — what memory care includes and who it fits

Care needs usually grow over time. Ask every community what happens when your parent needs more care than their current level — moving twice is hard on everyone. See levels of care explained.

Lifestyle & Services

Every community sets its own rules on pets, couples moving in together, and age requirements — and its own menu of medical and non-medical services, including transportation. Ask for the current policies and a full service list in writing; details here can change and anything unlisted should be confirmed on your tour.

Do you work at Merrill Gardens at Rockridge?

Families make one of their biggest decisions with pages like this one — and nobody can describe your community better than the people who work there. If you're on staff or manage this community, you can update these details, answer short topic-by-topic questionnaires (each takes just a few minutes), and share photos of your community. It's free, and accurate listings help the right families find you.

Claim & update this listing

Ratings & official records

Here is every rating we found for Merrill Gardens at Rockridge — who assigns each one and what it actually measures — so you can weigh it for what it's worth. No single number decides a good fit; use these to build questions, then trust your own visit.

State-
licensed

State license & inspections

Who assigns it: California — not Medicare. Assisted living, memory care, and small care homes have no federal star rating; the state licenses and inspects them. What it covers: license status, inspection findings, and substantiated complaints.

Look up this community at the California Department of Social Services, Community Care Licensing Division · How to read the record fairly

Family
reviews

Family & resident reviews

Who writes it: families and residents, on public review sites (4.3/5 overall experience across 18 reviews on A Place for Mom (July 2026)). What it reflects: day-to-day life — food, friendliness, communication — not inspections. Read the recent ones and the middle-star ones, not just the average.

Read the reviews: A Place for Mom · Yelp — see the ratings and what families wrote, then judge for yourself.

How to read online reviews without being misled

We list communities that meet a verified family-review standard or hold a government rating of three stars or better — and we always show the rating with its source and date rather than hide it. How facilities are rated →

Senior Living Community is an independent information resource for families. We are not a referral service and do not provide medical, legal, or financial advice — for decisions about care, consult the professionals involved. Facility details change; always confirm directly with a community. Last data review: 2026-07-11. Run a senior living community? Claim your free listing or see our marketing services for communities.
